description: Will Crude Oil (CL) front month settlement price hit $120 by end of June 2026? Part of a multi-bracket crude oil event with targets at various price levels. The $120 strike is the most actively discussed threshold due to its connection to Iran conflict escalation. Uses CME official settlement price only — not intraday.
question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit $120 by end of June?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures equals or exceeds $120 on any trading day between market creation and the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise No.

triggers.description: CL active month official CME settlement price >= $120
triggers.metric: CME Crude Oil (CL) Active Month Settlement Price

resolution_logic.trigger_logic: THRESHOLD — any single day reaching $120 settlement triggers Yes
exclusions: Intraday trades, highs, lows do NOT count
Bids, offers, midpoint values, indicative prices do NOT count
Only official CME settlement price matters — which can differ from last traded price
Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays) are ignored
Settlement price corrections after initial publication are ignored
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: The Active Month roll is a key subtlety: the front month becomes non-active 2 business days before spot month expiration. If spot month expires Friday, next listed contract becomes active on Wednesday. This means the 'front month' can change mid-week, and different contracts may have different settlement prices. A WTI variant using Pyth oracle data (intraday 1-min candles) also exists — structurally different resolution despite the same $120 target.

catalyst_dates.event: CME CL settlement price published — any day could trigger resolution
US-Iran military escalation — primary supply disruption risk
FOMC meeting — rate decision affects dollar and commodity prices
Approximate May CL contract expiry — active month rolls to June

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Kalshi offers daily WTI price bracket markets (KXWTI series) — e.g., 'WTI oil price on Apr 1, 2026?' with multiple strike levels. These resolve to a specific price range on a specific day, while Polymarket's market is a cumulative 'ever reaches $120' over 3 months. A Kalshi trader would need to buy the $120+ bracket on every single trading day through June to replicate Polymarket's position. Structurally very different: Kalshi = daily snapshots, Polymarket = cumulative touch.

related_markets.notes: CL $120 by end of March — 1.45% Yes, about to resolve. Establishes near-term impossibility.
Ceasefire before oil hits $120 — directly uses this market's trigger as one of its resolution conditions
Ground invasion (65.5%) is the primary catalyst that could drive CL to $120 via supply disruption
$120 oil would accelerate recession probability — feedback loop between energy costs and economic outlook
